title: Osmolality-controlled fixation and simple preparation of human red blood cells for scanning electron microscopy
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6f709","text":"Scanning electron microscopy (SEM) provides a way to visualize red blood cell (RBC) morphology. Previous methods for human RBC fixation can induce osmotic-related changes to healthy RBCs, which can interfere with interpretation of biological morphological changes. In addition, traditional methods for fixation and dehydration of RBCs and associated SEM preparations involve multiple chemicals and time-intensive steps. Here, we provide a simplified protocol for human RBC fixation with careful control of osmolality. This protocol omits the use of sodium cacodylate, osmium tetroxide, and ethanol gradient dehydration steps, yet results in comparable outcomes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"cqp5g","text":"In order to obtain the human blood needed for these studies, ethical clearance must first be obtained.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"84hci","text":"Human blood carries a risk of possible transmission of bloodborne pathogens. steps: Suspend human RBCs in osmolality-controlled buffer Caution: Institutional bloodborne pathogen biosafety precautions should be followed when working with specimens containing unfixed human blood Add 25 µL of packed, washed RBCs to 500 µL osmolality-controlled buffer from Step #1 Mix well with pipette, gently

Dilute fixed, washed RBCs in dH 2 O Mix RBC pellet well with pipette and add 5 µL of fixed RBC pellet to 995 µL dH 2 O to create at 0.5% hematocrit (hct) suspension of washed and glutaraldehyde-fixed RBCs in water Mix well and add 100 µL 0.5% hct glutaraldehyde-fixed RBC suspension to 12mm round Poly-L-Lysine coated coverslide* on slide warmer *Use the most appropriate slide size for the specific scanning electron microscope that will be used to image cells Dry slides on slide warmer for 60 °C Overnight

Fix RBCs with osmolality-controlled glutarldehyde Add 500 µL osmolality-controlled 2% glutaraldehyde solution from Step #3 slowly to well-mixed RBC suspension from Step #2. Invert gently in microcentrifuge tube Incubate at Room temperature in the dark for 00:30:00 Final osmolality of fixing solution ≈ 260 mmol/kg Note: Osmolality measured with a Wescor VAPRO 5520 (vapor pressure osmometer)

Wash glutaraldehyde-fixed RBCs Centrifuge fixed RBCs after completion of Step #4 at 900 x g, , 5 minutes, reduced acceleration and braking (Acc: 4/9, Dec: 2/9) Remove supernatant without disturbing the RBC pellet Properly dispose of supernatant containing glutaraldehyde per institutional guidelines Add 1 mL dH 2 O and mix pellet well with pipette Centrifuge cells again 900 x g, °C, 5 minutes, reduced acceleration and braking (Acc: 4/9, Dec: 2/9) Remove supernatant without disturbing the RBC pellet

Note the following protocol includes volumes to fix one (1) sample of packed, washed human red blood cells (RBCs). For multiple samples, scale up all volumes proportionately Make osmolality-controlled buffer Add 300 µL 1X PBS (pH 7.4) + 200 µL distilled water (dH 2 O) in microcentrifuge tube for 500uL total osmolality-controlled buffer solution Mix well Osmolality ≈ 160 mmol/kg Note: Osmolality measured with a Wescor VAPRO 5520 (vapor pressure osmometer)

Prepare osmolality-controlled 2% glutaraldehyde solution Caution: Prepare glutaraldehyde solution in chemical fume hood Combine 240 µL dH 2 O, 240 µL 1X PBS (pH 7.4), 20 µL 50% glutaraldehyde in conical tube and mix well by inverting Osmolality ≈ 350 mmol/kg Note: Osmolality measured with a Wescor VAPRO 5520 (vapor pressure osmometer)
creator: Heather N Colvin
